mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, corporate revenue growth stagnates due to weak consumer purchasing power, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of observations when UNRATE stays above 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous expansion in industrial production alongside stable nonfarm payrolls indicates improving corporate capacity utilization without labor cost inflation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when INDPRO rises but PAYEMS declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales exceeding 2.0% signals immediate consumer demand acceleration, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in >50% of instances following a >2% RSAFS jump, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate renewed credit availability and consumer balance sheet repair, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when the economy is in a post-recession expansion phase.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns during post-recession expansions, or HOUST growth fails to correlate with SPX returns with Rank IC < 0.02 when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with persistently low policy rates compresses real yields and triggers expectations of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3% monthly while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls directly forecasts near-term corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 5-day SPX direction with Rank IC < 0.01, or returns are negative in >55% of positive payroll surprise months.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal deteriorating manufacturing demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in >60% of periods following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the threshold signal fails to separate bull and bear markets in walk-forward validation.
